response.reasoning: Candidate has 15+ years of software development experience, well beyond the 6+ year requirement. Strong full-stack background with TypeScript, Node.js, and React explicitly mentioned across multiple recent roles (re:cinq, Optilyz). Current role at re:cinq involves AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators, directly matching the AI agent development requirement. Has MongoDB and SQL/MySQL experience matching database requirements. Docker and Linux experience covers containerization basics. Strong TDD/testing background (Fluent-Asserts, Trial, Selenium course, TDD practice at RIDE) aligns with testing framework expertise. Java experience exists from Altom Consulting and National Instruments but is older (2014-2018); no explicit Kotlin or Nest.js mentioned. No explicit cloud platform mention beyond AWS CDK at Optilyz. Self-identifies as resume owner — note this is the candidate's own resume.
strengths: 15+ years full-stack experience with TypeScript, Node.js, React across multiple senior roles
Current AI-agent infrastructure and MCP work directly matches AI agent requirement
Strong testing/TDD background with own libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial) and Vitest-adjacent expertise
concerns: No explicit Nest.js experience mentioned in resume
Kotlin not mentioned; Java experience is several years old
Limited explicit cloud platform breadth — only AWS CDK referenced, no GCP/Azure or Kubernetes depth shown

key_factors.factor: Missing Nest.js and Kotlin in primary/secondary stack

response.justification: The candidate significantly exceeds the experience bar and matches the core primary stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React) with recent senior roles. Direct AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq is a rare and highly relevant match. Gaps in Nest.js and Kotlin are notable but learnable given the strong polyglot background (TypeScript, Java, DLang, C++, Swift, PHP). Testing discipline and architecture chops are well documented through OSS work and workshops.
